behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: A unit test with mocked endpoints could verify function calls, but the real failure boundary is the composition of three separate I/O operations into a single payload. If one data collection path gets dropped, overwritten, or misaligned during composition, a pure unit mock wouldn't catch it. We need to wire the three collection modules together and verify their combined output structure to catch composition errors that only appear when integrating real module behaviors.
behaviors.test_description: Test the data orchestration layer with test doubles for the three endpoints (metrics, issue tracker, deployment log). Verify that the composed payload passed to the summary function contains all three data sources with expected keys, using realistic response shapes. Simulate one source returning empty results to verify fallback handling.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: The real failure is graceful degradation under realistic failure conditions. A simple mock that returns an error wouldn't catch all real failure modes: timeouts, partial responses, malformed data, or connection refused. We need to test the orchestration layer's error handling with multiple modules wired together and test doubles that simulate realistic error conditions (not just exception throws). This catches the real failure where the code doesn't properly handle actual network/API failures.
behaviors.test_description: Test the data collection orchestration with realistic error injection: timeout on one endpoint, HTTP 500 on another, malformed response on a third. Verify each scenario produces a report with a `data unavailable` note for that section, that other sections are intact, and that the process completes without abort. Use test doubles that simulate network behavior (delay, connection errors) not just exception throws.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: If summary generation is deterministic (template-based or logic-driven string generation), a unit test with fixed inputs can verify that key values are present in output. The real failure is that a value from input data doesn't appear in the summary text, which is a pure function transformation problem. No I/O or scheduling needed. Note: if the summary uses an LLM call, this would escalate to Agentic level, but the issue description does not indicate LLM involvement.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test with fixed input data containing specific key values (metrics numbers, issue counts, deployment versions). Call the summary generation function and verify that each key value appears in the output narrative. Use parametrized tests covering different input configurations and edge cases (zero values, missing fields).

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: This behavior requires end-to-end verification of actual email delivery, not just mocking the email function. A mock email service catches 'was the code called?' but misses the real failure modes: SMTP configuration errors, network routing issues, authentication failures, TLS issues, email format problems (headers, MIME encoding), rate limiting, recipient filtering. The real failure boundary is actual message delivery, requiring either a real email service or a test SMTP server that verifies messages can be sent and received.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test using a test SMTP server (e.g., MailHog, fake SMTP) that verifies emails are actually sent with correct headers, subject, body, and recipient. Run the complete pipeline: collect data, generate summary, deliver email, query test mailbox to confirm message arrived. Verify email format is valid for production email clients.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: This behavior has two failure boundaries: (1) file is saved when email fails, and (2) retry happens on next scheduled run. The first is an Integration problem (filesystem I/O), the second is a System problem (scheduler behavior). The real failure is the retry not happening on the next run, which requires testing the scheduled job infrastructure itself. We need to verify the actual retry scheduling and file persistence across runs, not just that the fallback logic exists.
behaviors.test_description: System test that simulates two consecutive scheduled runs: first run with email service unavailable (mock/block SMTP), verify report file is saved with correct content; second run, verify the saved report is picked up and delivery is reattempted. Alternatively, test the scheduler directly to verify the retry job is registered and executes. Use a test filesystem or temp directory for file persistence across mock runs.
